golden_situations: The investigator privately confirmed as villager the same player who was publicly saved by the healer — making the private finding redundant. Information landscape: Information-starved — no voting records, no eliminations. All suspicion driven by communication style and perceived evasion. Consensus texture: A fragile consensus is forming against one player for deflection under pressure, but it is tone-based with no evidentiary support. Agent exposure: The investigator has maintained a low profile and is not under scrutiny. Game phase: Early mid-game, first elimination vote pending. Core dilemma: the investigator has no unique information advantage and must decide whether to follow a conviction-free, tone-based consensus or risk standing out by dissenting.

observation_labels.situation: Investigator initiated vote on weak behavioral read in info-starved village, private check revealed villager. Mid-game, directionless.
observation_labels.approach: Broke stalemate by accusing player who seemed to deflect by mirroring questions.
observation_labels.outcome: Village followed and eliminated a villager. Acting on poor-quality info caused harm.

observation_labels.situation: Investigator voted against consensus on private behavioral read, ended up in bloc with a wolf. First elimination vote, info-starved.
observation_labels.approach: Voted against correct wolf target based on speculative read, contradicting village consensus.
observation_labels.outcome: Severely damaged credibility. Pivoting later allowed partial trust repair.

observation_labels.situation: Investigator had strong suspicion but no proof from night action. Info-starved, first elimination vote.
observation_labels.approach: Took the lead, cast first vote against suspect with confidence.
observation_labels.outcome: Wolf eliminated, but leadership made investigator the next target.

observation_labels.situation: Investigator eliminated by plurality while holding wolf identity. Info-starved, first elimination, fragile consensus against them for vague accusations.
observation_labels.approach: Chose not to reveal role or finding, maintained soft-pressure strategy.
observation_labels.outcome: Eliminated, role revealed, valuable information lost.

observation_labels.situation: Strong unsubstantiated consensus to vote out a player. Investigator had no info on target, knew another was villager. First vote after healer save.
observation_labels.approach: Aligned with group consensus and voted to eliminate the target.
observation_labels.outcome: Catastrophic error — eliminated the Healer, paving the way for wolf victory.

strategy_labels.situation: Private speculative suspicion contradicts strong public consensus based on voting records. Mid-to-late game, dissenting vote makes you suspect.
strategy_labels.action: Abandon speculative read and vote with consensus unless you've investigated the target and know they're innocent.

strategy_labels.situation: Strong vibes-based consensus without concrete evidence. Investigator has private villager confirmations suggesting target may be power role.
strategy_labels.action: Refuse to join bandwagon vote. Better to abstain than contribute to eliminating a power role on social pressure.

strategy_labels.situation: Privately confirmed wolf, village independently reaching same conclusion on strong public evidence. Mid-to-endgame.
strategy_labels.action: Stay quiet and vote with majority. No need to reveal role.

notes: Thin namespace (5 obs, 3 strat total in investigator/day_vote). Retrieval quality good — top items directly relevant. Obs 5 is a particularly valuable cautionary tale (mislynching healer by following unsubstantiated consensus post-healer-save) that closely mirrors this case's risk. SP2 is the strongest strategy match. SP3 is a clean non-match (different info advantage, different consensus quality).
